macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Economy Shows Slow Growth Amid Rising Inflation and Middle East Tensions
items.summary: US GDP grew at 2% annualized rate in Q1, following weak prior quarter, but inflation exceeds Fed target due to oil prices spiked by Iran war. Economy described as 'hanging in there' despite headwinds, with conflicting data post-Fed's decision against rate cut.

items.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Hold Near Record Highs Despite Mixed Macro Data
items.summary: Last week, S&P 500 rose 0.29% to 7,230 and Nasdaq gained as tech stocks offset mixed economic news. Markets stable near highs entering May amid upcoming data releases.

items.title: Trump Accuses Iran Leadership of Disunity Amid Ongoing Conflict and Energy Crisis
items.summary: US President Trump criticizes Iran's leadership, hindering peace deal after nine-week conflict triggering global energy crisis. Investors eye upcoming US non-farm payrolls expected at 60,000 jobs and Fed speeches.

items.title: Pakistan Economy Strains Under Oil Surge and Inflation Nearing 11%
items.summary: Rising oil prices from Middle East crisis projected to push Pakistan inflation to 11% if oil hits $120/barrel, with GDP forecasts cut and stock market down 15% in Q1. Current account deficit may exceed $8B amid import reliance.

items.title: Weaker US Dollar Raises Costs for Americans on Imports and Travel
items.summary: US dollar down 10% since early 2025, quietly driving up prices for groceries, vacations, and imports. Contributes to broader inflationary pressures.

items.title: Spirit Airlines Ceases Operations After Failed $500M Bailout
items.summary: Budget carrier Spirit Airlines shuts down following failure to secure $500 million bailout from Trump administration. Marks significant corporate development in aviation sector.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitmain Increases ETH Staking to 83% of Holdings
items.summary: Bitmain has staked approximately 83% of its ETH holdings, up from 70%, marking a significant jump in the past day.
items.source: YouTube - I Called the AI Trade

items.title: US Military Actively Researching Bitcoin for National Defense
items.summary: A four-star admiral testified in Congress that the US military is researching Bitcoin as a weapon for national defense, including running a Bitcoin node and using proof-of-work to protect against Chinese cyberattacks.
items.source: YouTube - The Real Reason The US Government Is Hoarding Bitcoin

items.title: Bitcoin Nears Major Resistance at $86,000 with Bullish Momentum
items.summary: Bitcoin has broken above key resistance and is approaching $86,000 on the 3-day timeframe, with short-term bullish moves toward areas of liquidity.

items.title: Chainlink Price Bounces in $790-$1,000 Range with Key Supports
items.summary: Chainlink maintains strong support between $790-$850 and resistance at $950-$1,000, expected to range for weeks, with additional supports at $1.30-$1.32.

items.title: States Passing Bitcoin Reserve Bills; California Next
items.summary: Multiple states are advancing Bitcoin reserve legislation, with California potentially next, amid federal efforts for a strategic Bitcoin reserve.
items.source: YouTube - The Real Reason The US Government Is Hoarding Bitcoin

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: No New AI Model Releases in Last 24 Hours
items.summary: LLM Stats reports no new AI model releases or large language model updates from major labs like OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, and Mistral in the past 24 hours.

items.title: Samsung Introduces AI Features in Bespoke Appliances Powered by Google Gemini
items.summary: Samsung is quietly adding new AI capabilities to its Bespoke refrigerators and ovens using Google Gemini.

items.title: AI Disrupting Nursing with Gig-Work Apps Setting Pay and Schedules
items.summary: Gig-work apps for nurses are leveraging AI to evaluate performance, determine earnings, and manage work schedules, impacting the nursing industry.

items.title: Twilio's AI Strategy Drives Stock Surge
items.summary: Twilio's CEO credits AI initiatives for turning around the company, leading to a significant stock rise after previous growth challenges.

items.title: McKinsey Reports Strong ROI on AI Investments
items.summary: McKinsey indicates clients are achieving $3 in returns for every $1 invested in AI, signaling positive monetary outcomes.

items.title: ChatGPT 5.5 User Reports Return of Engaging Personality
items.summary: A user claims ChatGPT 5.5 has 'dropped the clipboard' and regained the personality of ChatGPT 4o.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nintendo Releases Update for Original Switch Ahead of Switch 2 Launch
items.summary: Nintendo has issued system update version 20.1.1 for the original Switch, fixing an outstanding issue just days before the Switch 2 launches on June 5.

items.title: Sega Files Trademark for Bonanza Bros. Golden Heist After 36 Years
items.summary: Sega has filed a new trademark in April 2026 for Bonanza Bros. Golden Heist, the 1990 side-scroller arcade game, hinting at a potential revival though no announcement or release window is confirmed.

items.title: Gaming News Roundup for May 3 Covers Xbox, Apex Legends, and More
items.summary: A daily gaming news video highlights updates on Xbox mode, Apex Legends, Krafton, and Girls Frontline developments from May 3.

items.title: New Game In Game Quest: The Backlog Battler Lets Players Fight Steam Backlog
items.summary: An upcoming game, In Game Quest: The Backlog Battler, challenges players to battle their Steam backlog, with enemies growing more dangerous based on spending.

items.title: Star Wars Battlefront 2 Sees Unexpected Resurgence in Popularity
items.summary: Star Wars Battlefront 2, once the lowest-rated in its series, has returned to popularity in recent weeks.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Maryland Becomes First US State to Ban Surveillance Pricing in Grocery Stores
items.summary: Maryland has enacted the first US law banning dynamic pricing based on consumer data in grocery stores, though critics note it includes carveouts. Other states like Colorado, California, Massachusetts, Illinois, and New Jersey are considering similar legislation.

items.title: Amadeus IT Group to Acquire Idemia Public Security for €1.2 Billion
items.summary: Spanish travel tech firm Amadeus, operator of the world's largest travel booking system, announced plans to buy French biometrics company Idemia Public Security in a €1.2B cash deal.

items.title: South Korea Launches Earth Observation Satellite No. 2 via SpaceX Falcon 9
items.summary: South Korea successfully launched its second Earth observation satellite for land monitoring and disaster response, alongside Foxconn's PEARL-1A and PEARL-1B low-Earth orbit satellites, using SpaceX's Falcon 9 from Vandenberg.

items.title: Palo Alto Networks Agrees to Acquire AI Gateway Startup Portkey for $120M-$140M
items.summary: Cybersecurity leader Palo Alto Networks is acquiring Portkey, a developer of AI gateway technology for managing and securing AI agents, in a deal valued at $120M-$140M according to sources.

items.title: Ask.com Shuts Down, Ending Era of Early AI Search Bot
items.summary: Ask.com, known as the original AI search bot and internet butler, has officially shut down.

items.title: Nissan Abandons Plans for US EV Plant Capable of 200,000 Vehicles Annually
items.summary: Nissan has scrapped its plans to build a US electric vehicle manufacturing plant that was set to produce 200,000 EVs per year.

items.title: Toto Pledges Investment in Ceramics Division to Address NAND Memory RAM Crisis
items.summary: Japanese toilet maker Toto is increasing investment in its ceramics production for NAND memory chips to help alleviate the ongoing RAM shortage.
